summary: Eval scenarios use flat tool call lists with per-scorer pass/fail expectations
context: Phase 16 context gathering: designing the YAML scenario format for the deterministic eval harness
rationale: Flat tool lists are simpler to write and maintain. Scorers infer patterns from sequences rather than relying on annotated step metadata. Per-scorer expectations (vs single pass/fail) allow scenarios to test mixed quality -- good sequencing but bad scope, for example.

alternatives.option: Annotated steps with rule references per tool call
alternatives.reason_rejected: More verbose, couples scenario format to rule IDs, harder to maintain as BEHAVIORS.md evolves
Too coarse -- can't express scenarios that are good on some dimensions and bad on others
